items.title: US inflation fell more than expected to 3.5% in June as petrol prices tumbled
items.description: Traders rein in bets on Fed rate rises as easing energy costs help tame price surges spurred by Middle East war

items.title: IBM shares plunge 25% as customers shift spending to AI
items.description: Chief executive Arvind Krishna says company ‘faltered’ as clients raced to buy servers and storage

items.title: Wall Street banks smash records on stock trading boom
items.description: Earnings at JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Citigroup and BofA benefit from AI frenzy and SpaceX IPO

items.title: Trump says US to abandon proposed Strait of Hormuz cargo fee
items.description: President says he will ‘replace’ plan with investment from Gulf states into the US

items.title: New York becomes first US state to suspend data centre development
items.description: Governor Kathy Hochul signs one-year moratorium on new construction amid backlash over infrastructure needs

items.title: Kevin Warsh vows Federal Reserve will be ‘resolute’ in inflation fight
items.description: Central bank chief says he will not declare ‘mission accomplished’ after report showing lower price growth

items.title: Ukraine targets Russian shipping in ‘unprecedented’ drone assault
items.description: Over the past week, Kyiv has struck vessels in the Sea of Azov at a faster pace than during the 1980s Iran-Iraq tanker wars

items.title: Buffett drops Gates Foundation from $6bn Berkshire donation
items.description: Billionaire chair of conglomerate shifts charity plans after revelations about Microsoft founder’s Epstein ties

items.title: Washington and Tehran send warnings by missile
items.description: The foes are in their most dangerous period of fighting since a fragile April truce. Many of their strikes are designed to send signals

items.title: Democrats are not in great shape
items.description: A fractious opposition is doing everything it can to spurn an open goal

items.title: Google backs major US solar project to offset fossil fuel emissions
items.description: Deal signals continued demand for renewable energy despite Trump administration’s efforts to end tax credits and stall plans
